mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, which forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows or positive forward returns dominate during CPIAUCSL uptrends.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals an impending economic contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS indicates a synchronized collapse in housing demand and consumer credit, which amplifies negative wealth effects and reduces aggregate spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC below 0.02 or positive returns occur in more than 60% of co-declining periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient production capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, supporting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrends during tightening periods yield negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0%, ultra-low policy rates signal severe economic weakness or deflationary pressures, prompting risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS drops below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline, where job additions stall while unemployment falls, indicates labor force participation deterioration and weak underlying demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC below 0.02 or positive returns dominate in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding GDPC1 reflects robust economic growth that boosts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows or GDPC1 expansion periods yield negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO drops below 100.0, industrial production contraction signals a broad economic slowdown that reduces capital utilization and earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100.0.
